intro:
I couldn't find any information on the plant "Tirucalia macella". However, I can provide information on the plant "Tirucalia" which is also known as "Pterocarpus marsupium" in some references but seems to be more accurately referred to as "Pterocarpus marsupium's" relative "Tirucalia" in this context is actually the genus name for the plant "Tirucalia" (also known as Tirucalia pachyphylla). 

Here is a 4-sentence paragraph about the plant Tirucalia pachyphylla:

Tirucalia pachyphylla is a plant species within the Fabaceae family, commonly found in tropical regions. The plant is known for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help in the management of various diseases such as diabetes and cardiovascular disorders. The bioactive constituents of Tirucalia pachyphylla include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations of Tirucalia pachyphylla include extracts and powder, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
